title: Puerto Banus Family Boat Rental: Costs & Tips for 2026
html:
For a family day out on the water, renting a boat in Puerto Banus offers sun, sea and style. Departing from the iconic marina beside the Club de Mar, a four-hour motorboat trip for a family of six typically starts around 450 EUR. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is the main departure point, with its central marina housing dozens of charter companies. The nearby port of Marbella, just 3 nautical miles east, offers alternative berths. For a longer day, head towards the Cabopino marina or the secluded bay of Elviria. The natural reserve of the Dunes of Artola lies a short cruise west. Most family trips also explore the waters off Estepona, 10 nautical miles south-west.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24’s platform lists a range of vessel types. Below are the most popular choices for a family day.

  1. Speedboat – Ideal for short trips and watersports, seats up to 8 guests, from 300 EUR for a half-day.
  2. Motor yacht – A comfortable option with cabins, for families of up to 10, from 700 EUR for a full day. See the Fairline Targa 43 listing for a popular model.
  3. Catamaran – Stable and spacious, perfect for larger groups of up to 12, from 900 EUR for a full day.
  4. RIB boat – Fast and practical, suited for 6 passengers, from 400 EUR for 4 hours.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

A half-day motorboat rental for a family of six typically costs between 450 and 650 EUR. Prices vary by vessel and season. The breakdown below gives typical costs for 2026.

  • Shared group tour (2–3 hours): from 80 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat, half-day (4 hours): 450–750 EUR.
  • Catamaran, full day (8 hours): 900–1,500 EUR.
  • Crewed yacht (8–12 guests, full day): 1,200–2,500 EUR.

For the best choice, compare verified boat rental Puerto Banus options on BoatHire24 before booking.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for most boats under 15 kW (20 hp) in Spanish waters, but rules vary for larger vessels. Operators on BoatHire24 specify licence requirements for each boat. The high season runs from June to August, when the easterly Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon. One local tip: set off early to enjoy calm seas before the breeze strengthens.

Book a 10:00 am departure to avoid midday heat and afternoon wind; the strait can get choppy after 2 pm in summer.

At a glance

  • Price range: 450–2,500 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: 4–8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6–12 persons
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)
  • Popular models: Fairline Targa 43, Azimut 55

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can you rent a boat in Puerto Banus without a licence?

Yes, you can hire small motorboats and RIBs below 15 kW without a licence, but always confirm with the operator.

What is the best time of year for a family boat trip in Puerto Banus?

Late spring (May) and early autumn (September) offer warm weather, fewer crowds and calmer seas than July and August.

How far is Marbella from Puerto Banus by boat?

Marbella marina is about 3 nautical miles south-east, a 20-minute cruise at slow speed.

Is it safe to take children on a boat rental in Puerto Banus?

Yes, most family charters provide life jackets for all ages and the sheltered bay waters are generally calm.

Whether you are planning a half-day outing or a full-week exploration, booking through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified vessels and clear pricing. Owners in the area can also list your boat on BoatHire24 to reach families seeking the perfect day on the Mediterranean.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
